messages.content: In conclusion, this research endeavors to provide a legal framework that dissects the complex relationship between terrorism and organized crime. By elucidating the distinctions and similarities, it aims to contribute to the ongoing discourse on refining legal instruments, fostering international collaboration, and fortifying the defenses against threats that transcend geographical and jurisdictional borders. ترجم للعربي

في الختام، تسعى هذه الدراسة إلى توفير إطار قانوني يحلل العلاقة المعقدة بين الإرهاب والجريمة المنظمة. ومن خلال توضيح الاختلافات والتشابهات، تهدف إلى المساهمة في النقاش المستمر حول تحسين الأدوات القانونية، وتعزيز التعاون الدولي، وتقوية الدفاعات ضد التهديدات التي تتجاوز الحدود الجغرافية والقضائية.
بحث الحدود الفاصلة بين الأرهاب والجريمة المنظمة ، اقترح عليا نهاية مميزة للبحث كخاتمة للبحث بعد

في ختام هذا البحث الذي دقق في تشابكات وتمايزات مسارات الأرهاب والجريمة المنظمة، نتطلع إلى أن نكون قد أضأنا على زوايا قد تظل غامضة في هذا المعترك المتشابك. ونأمل بأن تسهم الرؤى المستخلصة هنا في بلورة مناهج قانونية متينة وملائمة تواكب التطورات الحديثة وتوفر أساسًا متينًا للتعاون الدولي الفعّال. وليكن هذا العمل بمثابة دعوة للعمل المشترك والعزيمة الثابتة في مواجهة الأخطار التي لا تعترف بحدود ولا تقيم وزنًا للسيادات، بل تعمل على نسف أسس المجتمعات الآمنة والمستقرة. وفي الأخير، لا يسعنا إلا أن نؤكد على أهمية استباق الأحداث ومواصلة الجهود بلا كلل لضمان سلامة وأمان الإنسانية جمعاء.
وايه كمان

في ضوء ما سبق، يُعد تعزيز التعاون القضائي والأمني الدولي محوراً رئيسياً لكسر حلقات التواصل بين الإرهاب والجريمة المنظمة. بالتوازي، يُشدد هذا البحث على الحاجة الماسة لتطوير استراتيجيات شاملة تضمن الاستجابة السريعة والفاعلة للتهديدات المستجدة، وذلك عبر استحداث معايير تشريعية وأطر رقابية تتسم بالمرونة والتكيف مع الطبيعة المتحولة للجريمة العابرة للحدود.

لا يقتصر الأمر على مواجهة التحديات القائمة فحسب، بل يجب أن نركز كذلك على الوقاية والتثقيف. إذ يبقى تعزيز وعي المواطنين بخطورة هذه الجرائم وآثارها المدمرة ركيزة أساسية، إضافة إلى نشر الثقافة القانونية والأمنية بما يكفل تحصين المجتمعات.

ومن الضروري أيضاً تمكين الأجهزة القانونية والأمنية من خلال التدريب المستمر وتطوير قدرات كوادرها وتزويدها بأحدث التقنيات اللازمة للتصدي لهذه التحديات. كما يجب علينا الاعتراف بأهمية البحث العلمي والأكاديمي في استشراف ملامح التهديدات المستقبلية وتصميم الاستراتيجيات الوقائية.

بهذه المقاربات المتعددة الأبعاد والجهود المشتركة، نضع حجر الأساس لبناء منظومة دولية قوية قادرة على الحد من الجرائم المنظمة والأعمال الإرهابية، لنصنع بذلك مستقبلاً أكثر أمانًا وازدهاراً للأجيال القادمة.

لنختم بالقول إن طريق العدالة يطلب منا جميعاً اليقظة والمثابرة، وبأنه مهما كانت التحديات جسيمة، فعزيمتنا على بناء عالم خالٍ من ظلال الإرهاب وآفات الجريمة يجب ألا تفتر.

كما نتطلع إلى أن نكون قد أضأنا على زوايا قد تظل غامضة في هذا المعترك المتشابك. ونأمل بأن تسهم الرؤى المستخلصة هنا في بلورة مناهج قانونية متينة وملائمة تواكب التطورات الحديثة وتوفر أساسًا متينًا للتعاون الدولي الفعّال. وليكن هذا العمل بمثابة دعوة للعمل المشترك والعزيمة الثابتة في مواجهة الأخطار التي لا تعترف بحدود ولا تقيم وزنًا للسيادات، بل تعمل على نسف أسس المجتمعات الآمنة والمستقرة. وفي الأخير، لا يسعنا إلا أن نؤكد على أهمية استباق الأحداث ومواصلة الجهود بلا كلل لضمان سلامة وأمان الإنسانية جمعاء ترجم للانجليزي ترجمة قانونية احترافية

In conclusion, we aspire to have shed light on aspects that may remain obscure within this intricate battlefield of terror and organized crime. We hope that the insights gleaned here contribute to the formulation of robust and suitable legal methodologies that keep pace with modern developments, providing a solid foundation for effective international cooperation. Let this work serve as a call to collective action and unwavering resolve in the face of dangers that recognize no borders and hold no esteem for sovereignty, dangers that threaten to undermine the foundations of safe and stable societies. Ultimately, we must emphasize the importance of being proactive and tirelessly continuing our efforts to ensure the safety and security of humanity as a whole.

في ضوء ما سبق، يُعد تعزيز التعاون القضائي والأمني الدولي محوراً رئيسياً لكسر حلقات التواصل بين الإرهاب والجريمة المنظمة. بالتوازي، يُشدد هذا البحث على الحاجة الماسة لتطوير استراتيجيات شاملة تضمن الاستجابة السريعة والفاعلة للتهديدات المستجدة، وذلك عبر استحداث معايير تشريعية وأطر رقابية تتسم بالمرونة والتكيف مع الطبيعة المتحولة للجريمة العابرة للحدود.

In light of the foregoing, strengthening international judicial and security cooperation is a primary axis for breaking the nexus between terrorism and organized crime. Concurrently, this research emphasizes the urgent need to develop comprehensive strategies that ensure rapid and effective response to emerging threats, by establishing flexible legislative standards and regulatory frameworks that adapt to the evolving nature of transnational crime.

ومن الضروري أيضاً تمكين الأجهزة القانونية والأمنية من خلال التدريب المستمر وتطوير قدرات كوادرها وتزويدها بأحدث التقنيات اللازمة للتصدي لهذه التحديات. كما يجب علينا الاعتراف بأهمية البحث العلمي والأكاديمي في استشراف ملامح التهديدات المستقبلية وتصميم الاستراتيجيات الوقائية.

بهذه المقاربات المتعددة الأبعاد والجهود المشتركة، نضع حجر الأساس لبناء منظومة دولية قوية قادرة على الحد من الجرائم المنظمة والأعمال الإرهابية، لنصنع بذلك مستقبلاً أكثر أمانًا وازدهاراً للأجيال القادمة.

لنختم بالقول إن طريق العدالة يطلب منا جميعاً اليقظة والمثابرة، وبأنه مهما كانت التحديات جسيمة، فعزيمتنا على بناء عالم خالٍ من ظلال الإرهاب وآفات الجريمة يجب ألا تفتر

It is also essential to empower legal and security apparatuses through continuous training, capacity building for their personnel, and equipping them with the latest technologies required to confront these challenges. Furthermore, we must acknowledge the significance of scientific and academic research in anticipating the contours of future threats and in designing preventive strategies.

With these multidimensional approaches and collaborative efforts, we lay the foundation stone for constructing a robust international system capable of curtailing organized crime and terrorist activities, thereby carving out a safer and more prosperous future for the coming generations.

Let us conclude by stating that the path to justice demands vigilance and perseverance from us all, and regardless of the severity of the challenges ahead, our determination to build a world free from the shadows of terrorism and the scourges of crime must not wane.
Type, method or approach ترجم للعربي
نوع، طريقة أو منهج

The persisting links between organized crime and terrorism have resulted in an array of theories trying to explain the crime-terror nexus. Over the years, the understanding of the relationship between organized crime and terrorism has evolved. The definition of both the concepts of ‘organized crime’ and ‘terrorism’ is subject to extensive debate. It is important to note that these concepts can overlap, and any definition has its limitations ترجم للعربي

أدت الروابط المستمرة بين الجريمة المنظمة والإرهاب إلى ظهور مجموعة من النظريات التي تحاول تفسير تلازم الجريمة والإرهاب. على مر السنين، تطورت فهم العلاقة بين الجريمة المنظمة والإرهاب. إن تعريف كلا المفهومين 'الجريمة المنظمة' و'الإرهاب' يخضع لنقاش واسع. من المهم ملاحظة أن هذه المفاهيم يمكن أن تتداخل، وأن أي تعريف لها قيوده.

The persisting links between organized crime and terrorism have resulted in an array of theories trying to explain the crime-terror nexus. Over the years, the understanding of the relationship between organized crime and terrorism has evolved. The definition of both the concepts of ‘organized crime’ and ‘terrorism’ is subject to extensive debate. It is important to note that these concepts can overlap, and any definition has its limitations اعملي اعادة صياغة للفقرة دي بالانجليزي

Enduring connections between organized crime and terrorism have given rise to various theories aiming to delineate the interfaces of the crime-terror continuum. As time has progressed, our comprehension of the ties between organized crime and terrorism has advanced. Defining the terms 'organized crime' and 'terrorism' remains a matter of considerable scholarly discussion. It is critical to acknowledge that there can be intersections between these notions, and that all definitions are inherently constrained by their scope.

The persisting links between organized crime and terrorism have resulted in an array of theories trying to explain the crime-terror nexus. Over the years, the understanding of the relationship between organized crime and terrorism has evolved. The definition of both the concepts of ‘organized crime’ and ‘terrorism’ is subject to extensive debate. It is important to note that these concepts can overlap, and any definition has its limitations ترجم للعربي

لقد أدت الروابط المستمرة بين الجريمة المنظمة والإرهاب إلى نشوء مجموعة من النظريات التي تسعى إلى تفسير علاقة الجريمة بالإرهاب. وعلى مر السنين، تطورت الفهم للعلاقة بين الجريمة المنظمة والإرهاب. يخضع تعريف مفهومي 'الجريمة المنظمة' و'الإرهاب' لنقاش موسع. من المهم الإشارة إلى أن هذه المفاهيم قد تتقاطع، وأن أي تعريف له قيوده الخاصة.

Therefore, he discusses Policing Terrorism by Newman and Clarke (2008), and confronts it with experiences from work in the field of counterterrorism: criminal investigations at the Dutch National Police Agency. He criticizes Newman and Clarke of having a too narrow scope on policing terrorism, in particular when it comes to the role of criminal investigation, as they restrict their focus to local police work in the phases immediately before and after a terrorist attack. According to the author, case files show that criminal investigators play a key role in the prevention of terrorism. They intervene in earlier phases of terrorist preparation, target a wider range of terrorist offences than terrorist attacks, and prosecute radicals for their non-terrorist offences. Furthermore, criminal investigations offer insights that might be of help in developing a situational crime prevention approach to terrorism اعملي اعادة صياغة للفقرة دي

Thus, he engages with the concepts presented in "Policing Terrorism" by Newman and Clarke (2008), contrasting their views against real-world experiences from his work in counterterrorism and criminal investigative roles at the National Police Agency of the Netherlands. He levels criticism at Newman and Clarke for what he sees as their overly limited perspective on terrorism policing, especially concerning the significance of criminal investigations, which they confine largely to the immediate pre- and post-terrorist attack timeframe. The author contends that, based on case file analysis, criminal investigators are pivotal in thwarting terrorism, by stepping in during the earlier stages of terrorist planning, addressing a broader spectrum of terrorist-related offenses beyond just attacks, and pursuing prosecution of extremists based on non-terrorist crimes. Moreover, he suggests that insights gained from criminal investigations could be valuable in shaping strategies for situational crime prevention tailored to terrorism.

are employed in the production of data about TOC which, in turn, justifies their raison d’être, growth and increased funding and thus bolsters the careers of staff in these organizations. In a competitive funding environment, it is easy for actors within the anti-TOC arena to become forces for themselves, acting in the interest of their own survival, relevance and influence. This is not only the case for international and government agencies, but also for an increasing amount of private and semi-private companies and institutes, many of which are closely linked with the security industry عدلي صياغة الفقرة دي

They are utilized in generating information about transnational organized crime (TOC), which consequently validates their existence, expansion, and the arguments for increased financial support, thereby enhancing the career prospects of individuals working within these entities. In the context of a competitive fiscal milieu, it becomes tempting for those involved in combating TOC to evolve into self-serving entities, focused on their own preservation, pertinence, and sway. This phenomenon isn't confined to international and governmental organizations but extends to a growing number of private and semi-private firms and think tanks, many of which maintain strong ties to the security sector.

Despite the fact that much of what terrorists do is already covered by “ordinary” legislation, in a number of countries specialized crimes have been created in order to deal with behaviors which are more or less unique to terrorism. These include training for terrorist purposes, membership in a terrorist organization, possession of terrorist-related documents or other materials, preparing or conspiring to commit an act of terrorism, encouragement, incitement or glorification of terrorism, fundraising for terrorism (which might otherwise involve legal means), and providing material support to terrorist organizations. Some of these offences have very close parallels in ordinary legislation – for example, incitement of hatred or violence, and conspiracy. However, the “terrorist” component ties these offences to legal definitions of terrorism and specific,
designated organizations. Together, specialized terrorism offences enable prosecution of a broader range of behaviors and often denote added severity (and therefore harsher punishments) for actions which may still have a rough equivalent in ordinary criminal law. Perhaps most importantly, specialized legislation facilitates pre-emptive arrest and prosecution before an attack takes place
عيد صياغة الفقرة دي

Although a significant portion of terrorist activities falls under the purview of general criminal laws, several countries have enacted specialized offenses to address actions that are somewhat distinctive to terrorism. These specialized crimes encompass a range of behaviors such as undergoing training for terrorist activities, affiliating with a terrorist group, possessing materials linked to terrorism, preparing or plotting a terrorist act, promoting, inciting, or endorsing terrorism, funding terrorism (potentially through lawful means), and supplying resources to terrorist entities. Some of these crimes closely mirror existing laws like incitement to hatred or violence and conspiracy, yet the addition of the "terrorism" label connects these acts to formal definitions of terrorism and particular identified groups. Specialized terrorism-related laws allow for the legal pursuit of a wider spectrum of conduct and often imply increased gravity, leading to stricter penalties compared to their counterparts in standard criminal legislation. Perhaps the most critical aspect of these laws is that they empower authorities to make preemptive arrests and pursue legal action prior to the execution of a terrorist attack.

The FBI defines organized crime as any group having some manner of a formalized structure and whose primary objective is to obtain money through illegal activities. Such groups maintain their position through the use of actual or threatened violence, corrupt public officials, graft, or extortion, and generally have a significant effect on the people in their locales, region, or countries اعلي اعادة صياغة للفقرة دي

The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) characterizes organized crime as a collective that possesses some level of formal hierarchy whose main goal is to generate profit through illicit endeavors. These entities secure their influence by deploying real or intimidating force, compromising governmental officials, engaging in fraudulent activities, or practicing extortion. They often exert a profound impact on the communities, regions, or nations in which they operate.

Of course, every country has its own ‘triggering events’ and its own national history, yet several broader ‘European’ developments might account for coordinated waves of concern and response across countries: the murders of the Italian judges Falcone and Borsellino in 1992, which received widespread media attention throughout Europe; the emergence of highly profitable transnational drug trafficking; globalization; diminishing border controls within the European Union; the fall of the Iron Curtain; and the emergence of the smuggling of illegal immigrants and human trafficking اعمل اعادة صياغة للفقرة دي

Certainly, each nation has distinct 'catalytic incidents' and a unique historical context, but there are common 'European' dynamics that can explain unified periods of alarm and action across different countries. These include the high-profile assassinations of Italian magistrates Falcone and Borsellino in 1992, which were prominently reported across Europe; the rise of lucrative international narcotics trade; the effects of globalization; the easing of border checks within the European Union; the collapse of the Eastern bloc; and the increase in the illicit transportation of migrants and the trafficking of human beings.

Several terrorist organizations have become so successful in their criminal activities that they are often accused of being just as, if not more, concerned with making money than achieving their ideological goals. In the case of the Afghan Taliban, “[s]ome observers argue that that the line between ‘the definition of Taliban member and drug smuggler is blurring’ as Taliban commanders become more directly involved in the trade—running their own heroin laboratories, for example—and are more motivated by profits and power than ideological considerations. In this way, the definitional boundaries between ‘insurgent’ and ‘organized criminal group’ begin to collapse عيد صياغة االفقرة دي

A number of terrorist groups have grown so adept at their illicit operations that they frequently face accusations of prioritizing financial gain over their stated ideological objectives. Regarding the Afghan Taliban, it is stated by some analysts that the distinctions are becoming less clear, as '[the terms] Taliban member and narcotics trafficker are increasingly intermingling.' This is due to Taliban leaders stepping directly into the narcotics business—running heroin processing labs, for instance—and being driven more by profit and control than by ideological imperative. Consequently, the lines that define 'rebel' and 'organized crime syndicate' are beginning to merge.

By comparison with the prevention of terrorism, the prevention of acts of organized crime might be thought easier to conceptualize precisely and less controversial to legislate against and police. This impression is correct up to a point, because it is possible to arrive at some general characteristics of organized crime, and because legislation against it is not currently subject to heated public controversy based on probable violations of civil or political rights, as in the case of terrorism. But there is a significant residue of legal, moral and political difficulty: legislation against organized crime is hard to make effective; the harm of organized crime is not uniform, and so some preventive legislation seems too sweeping and potentially unjust. More fundamentally, the scale and rewards of organized crime are often dependent on mass public participation in markets for proscribed goods, which may point to a hidden public consensus in favour of some of what is criminalized. Preventive policing and legislation in both areas, then, are less easily justified than first appears عيد صياغة الفقرة دي

When contrasted with countering terrorism, the prevention of organized crime might appear more straightforward to define accurately and less contentious to legislate and enforce. This perception holds true to some extent because it is feasible to identify key characteristics of organized crime, and because current laws against it do not typically provoke intense public debates over potential infringements of civil or political rights, as is often the case with anti-terrorism laws. However, there remains a considerable degree of legal, ethical, and political complexity: enforcing laws against organized crime can be challenging; the impact of organized crime varies, leading to preventive laws that may appear excessively broad and might risk being unfair. At a more fundamental level, the extent and profits of organized crime frequently rely on widespread public engagement in illicit markets, suggesting an underlying societal acceptance of some activities that have been criminalized. Consequently, the justification for preventive policing and legislation in both domains is more nuanced than it initially seems.

In addition, terrorist groups are willing to use organized crime networks often characterized by specialization, division of labor and decentralization. It makes their structure more effective. Organized criminals have expanded their networks further owing to globalization that, in turn, makes it easy for terrorist groups to move and acquire goods and services in different parts of the world. Terrorists need more of these goods and services as they grow, while they also learn the importance of decentralization from criminal enterprises. Since 9 / 11 attacks many terrorist groups have had their funds frozen, and they started to look to criminal enterprises for generating revenue and making transactions without creating too many red flags. The organized criminals have found a consumer market of their goods and services in the form of terrorist groups. Smaller criminal gangs are even more willing to partner with terrorist groups to establish themselvesعيد صياغة الفقرة دي

Furthermore, terrorist organizations are inclined to capitalize on the traits of organized crime networks, which are often marked by specialization, division of work, and decentralization—factors that enhance their operational efficiency. Thanks to globalization, criminal networks have broadened their reach, simplifying the process for terrorist groups to transport and obtain goods and services globally. As terrorist organizations expand, their demand for these goods and services increases, and they also adopt the principle of decentralization evident in criminal enterprises. Following the financial clampdown post-9/11, with the freezing of their assets, numerous terror groups have turned to criminal operations as a means of revenue generation and to conduct transactions discreetly. In doing so, they provide a ready market for the products and services of organized crime. Even smaller criminal factions are more inclined to collaborate with terrorist organizations to solidify their own foothold.

3.	Terrorist groups typically acknowledge their acts while refusing to recognize them as crimes; they use courtrooms to present their case, appearing as heroes and making political statements. In contrast, organized crime groups attempt to defend themselves and downplay their involvement in criminal activity. عيد صياغة الفقرة دي

3. It is characteristic of terrorist organizations to claim responsibility for their actions yet reject the label of criminality; they exploit legal proceedings as a platform to justify their cause, casting themselves as martyrs and voicing political proclamations. On the other hand, entities involved in organized crime usually strive to minimize their criminal culpability and present defenses to mitigate their association with illicit conduct.
For this reason, they offer a sacrifice to society, even if it means sacrificing themselves عيد صايغة دي
Consequently, they present themselves as a sacrifice for the greater good of society, even to the extent of self-sacrifice.

5.	Furthermore, while crime and terrorism are very much transnational, CT and LE responses still tend to be predominantly national and are limited by a continuing reluctance to share information across national jurisdictions, particularly when it is classified.
 National police forces also remain hard pressed to counter terrorists and OCGs that are sometimes better equipped, better armed and more agile than themselves. There is thus a clear need for improved interagency and international cooperation and capacity building
عيد صيافة الفقرة دي

5. Moreover, despite the cross-border nature of crime and terrorism, counterterrorism (CT) and law enforcement (LE) efforts are often confined within national boundaries. This limitation is further exacerbated by a persistent hesitance to exchange information across national lines, especially when dealing with classified data.

National law enforcement agencies frequently find themselves at a disadvantage, struggling to match the resources, weaponry, and agility of terrorists and organized crime groups (OCGs). This disparity highlights an evident need for enhanced collaborative efforts between agencies and countries, as well as for bolstering international cooperation and capability development.

Whether a State is attempting to prevent organized crime activities, financial and economic crime, corruption or terrorism, the establishment of better legal bases for international cooperation is a prerequisite1. Strengthening the convergence of criminal law and criminal law procedure is part of any long-term strategy to build more effective international cooperation. Developing stronger bilateral and multi-lateral agreements on mutual legal assistance is also part of the solution عيد صياغة الفقرة دي

When a state endeavors to curb activities such as organized crime, financial and economic fraud, corruption, or terrorism, laying down more robust legal grounds for international cooperation is fundamental. Solidifying the alignment of criminal statutes and procedural law constitutes a key element of any enduring strategy aimed at enhancing global collaboration. Moreover, forging more substantial bilateral and multilateral agreements concerning mutual legal support forms an integral piece of the overarching resolution.

The universal conventions against terrorism, as well as the United Nations Conventions against Transnational Organized Crime and against Corruption provide a strong basis for legal cooperation and often suggest some of the elements that must be developed as part of a national capacity for effective investigation and prosecution of these crimes. Having national legislation in place to fully implement these instruments is therefore of paramount importance. as is the adoption of the administrative measures necessary to build strong prosecution services and support the various modalities of both domestic coordination and international cooperation عيد صياغة الفقرة دي

Global treaties targeting terrorism, along with the United Nations Conventions aimed at dismantling Transnational Organized Crime and combating Corruption, establish a robust framework for judicial collaboration, often indicating essential components required for nations to develop capable investigative and prosecutorial practices for these crimes. Therefore, it is critically essential for nations to enact domestic laws that fully actualize the provisions of these international instruments. Equally important is the implementation of administrative actions that underpin potent prosecutorial bodies and foster the diverse approaches needed for both national coordination and cross-border cooperation.

At this stage, it is worth reminding ourselves that fighting TOC has become a transnational business. By that we mean a business for the agencies, companies and consultancy firms that have emerged in order to cater to the perceived security needs generated by the ever-expanding framing of TOC as a threat .many of these agencies - like UNODC, EMCDDA and EUROPOL – are utilized in generating information about transnational organized crime (TOC), which consequently validates their existence, expansion, and the arguments for increased financial support, thereby enhancing the career prospects of individuals working within these entities. عيد صياغة الفقرة دي

At this juncture, it's pertinent to recall that combating Transnational Organized Crime (TOC) itself has evolved into a global industry. This refers to the proliferation of agencies, corporations, and consultancies that have surfaced to meet the growing demand for security services, a demand driven by the intensifying portrayal of TOC as a significant menace. Various agencies, such as UNODC, EMCDDA, and EUROPOL, are engaged in the production of data on TOC. This activity not only justifies their continued operation and growth, and bolsters their appeal for increased funding, but it also potentially boosts the career trajectories of their personnel.

It has been established that proactive security strikes are one of the most effective tools in confronting both terrorist crimes and organized crime alike, and therefore Weenink, in his article on situational prevention of terrorism, shows how criminal investigation might contribute to a situational approach to terrorism عيد صياغة الفقرة دي

The effectiveness of preemptive security measures in tackling both acts of terrorism and organized crime has been recognized. Correspondingly, Weenink demonstrates in his article on the situational prevention of terrorism the pivotal role that criminal investigation can play in adopting a situational strategy to counter terrorism.

Concerns about organized crime are of a relatively recent date. In many European countries, organized crime began to be seen as a serious problem only in the late 1980s or early 1990s. عيد صياغة الفقرة دي

Anxiety over organized crime has surfaced relatively late in history. It was not until the late 1980s or early 1990s that organized crime started to be perceived as a significant concern in numerous European nations.
